TopModule.demo_behavior:
Module: TopModule I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise specified. - input  y (6 bits) - input  w - output Y1 - output Y3 Consider the state machine shown below: A (0) --0--> B A (0) --1--> A B (0) --0--> C B (0) --1--> D C (0) --0--> E C (0) --1--> D D (0) --0--> F D (0) --1--> A E (1) --0--> E E (1) --1--> D F (1) --0--> C F (1) --1--> D Resets into state A. For this part, assume that a one-hot code is used with the state assignment y[5:0] = 000001, 000010, 000100, 001000, 010000, 100000 for states A, B,..., F, respectively. The module shou module ment the next-state signals Y2 and Y4 corresponding to signal y[1] and y[3]. Derive the logic equations by inspection assuming the one-hot encoding. implement the next-state signals  and corresponding to signal y[1] and y[3]Derive the logic equations byinspection assuming the one-hot encoding.
Key rules: Implement behavior exactly as described in the prompt.
Reset: Only as explicitly described in the prompt.
Corner cases: No additional corner cases beyond prompt requirements.
Performance: Use prompt-defined timing/latency behavior.

test_goals: Pass benchmark harness checks for all provided tests.
verification.oracle_strategy: Use benchmark-provided reference outputs as oracle.
pass_fail_criteria: DUT outputs match oracle outputs for benchmark tests.
